resultCopy: Smoke clears within a few seconds of firing, an oil-burn smell, not coolant sweet. Points at the valve seals or the rings, not the head gasket.
Smoke keeps coming well past the first few seconds, faintly sweet. Points at the head gasket, not the seals or rings.
Compression reads even across every cylinder. The valve seals are the likely culprit, not the rings or the gasket.
One or more cylinders read low. Points at the rings or the head gasket, not the valve seals.

cardLine: Won't start. Turns over, nothing catches.

resultCopy: Cranking speed sags hard and the lights dim with it. The battery's the problem, not the pump or the engine itself.
Cranking speed and the lights both hold steady. Not the battery, narrows to the fuel pump or the engine itself.
The crank won't turn over by hand at all. The engine itself is seized, not a battery or fuel-pump fault.
The crank turns over freely by hand. Not a seized engine, narrows to the battery or the fuel pump.

cardLine: Faint tick at idle. Probably nothing.

resultCopy: A light, even tick right at the top of the head, in time with the valves. Just the lifters, not the bottom end or the exhaust.
The tick sources lower and further out than the valve gear. Not the lifters, narrows to the bottom end or a blowing manifold.
A gauge on the sender port shows oil pressure sagging low at hot idle, the knock deepening under load as it drops. Genuine bearing clearance in the bottom end, not the valve gear or the exhaust.
Hot-idle oil pressure holds steady and correct with the tick still there. The bottom end is sound, narrows to a worn lifter or a blowing manifold gasket.

cardLine: Won't hold an idle. Hunts and dies.

resultCopy: The idle changes the instant a hose joint gets a squirt. A vacuum leak, not the ECU, the cams or a cylinder gone down.
Nothing changes anywhere along the manifold. Not a vacuum leak, narrows to the ECU, the cam timing or a cylinder down on compression.
One cylinder reads badly down and won't hold pressure, the hiss carrying straight out of the exhaust. A burnt exhaust valve, not a vacuum leak or a tired ECU.
Compression reads even and healthy across every cylinder, every valve sealing. No burnt valve, and a squirt test aside, a yard gauge can take the ECU-versus-cams question no further.
Metal glints in the drained gearbox oil. A chewed gearset, not tired synchros or a clutch that won't let go.
The oil drains clean, just thin with age, nothing bright in it. Not a chewed gearset, narrows to worn synchros or a dragging clutch.
Engine running, pedal flat to the floor, and first still baulks and grates going in from a standstill. The clutch isn't fully releasing, dragging the input shaft, not a fault in the gears themselves.
Pedal flat to the floor, it drops into gear clean and silent from a standstill; the crunch only comes on the move. The clutch releases fully, narrows to worn synchros or a chewed gearset.

cardLine: Clunks over bumps at the back.

resultCopy: The corner keeps bouncing after a good shove instead of settling in one. The dampers are blown, not the bushes, the steering or anything structural.
The corner settles in a single rebound after a shove. Not the dampers, narrows to the bushes, the steering or a mounting point.
A screwdriver goes clean through the rear subframe mounting point, the metal round it laced with rust and flaking away. A rotted structural mount, not worn suspension parts.
The subframe mounts and rails are solid, sound metal all round when probed. Nothing structural, narrows to the dampers, the bushes or the steering.
Rocking the wheel at 3 and 9 with the front end up, there's a knock and lash back through the steering joints. Play in the steering, not the dampers or a mounting point.
Rocking the wheel at 3 and 9, the steering stays tight, no lash at the joints. Not the steering, narrows to the dampers, the bushes or a rotted mount.
The pads are shiny and glazed, but the calliper slides freely. Just the pads, not a seized calliper.
The calliper won't slide back freely on its pins. A seized calliper, not just the pads.

cardLine: Runs hot in traffic. Fine on the move.

resultCopy: Bubbles rise steadily in the header tank with the engine running, combustion gas pushing into the coolant. A breach between cylinder and water jacket, not the fan or the radiator.
The coolant stays clean, no bubbling as it warms. Not a combustion breach, narrows to the fan switch or a tired radiator.
The fan never kicks in even once the temp gauge climbs into the red. The fan switch, not the radiator or anything deeper.
The fan kicks in right on cue and still the heat builds in traffic. Not the fan switch, narrows to a tired radiator or a breach into the cooling system.

A cylinder reads badly down and holds no pressure, air hissing back through the coolant, the oil gone the colour of weak tea. The block itself is cracked or the head warped past the gasket, not a fault a service puts right.
Compression reads even and healthy across every cylinder, the oil clean on the stick. No cracked block, narrows to the fan, the radiator or an early gasket weep.

cardLine: Vibration through the wheel, builds with road speed.

resultCopy: Spun free of the ground, the tyre shows feathering across the tread and the wheel itself runs true. Worn tyres, not the rim or the driveshaft.
The tread wears evenly, nothing in the rubber to explain it. Not the tyres, narrows to a buckled rim or the driveshaft.
A split CV boot, grease flung round the inside of the arch. The driveshaft, not the tyres or the rim.
The boots are intact, no grease sling anywhere. Not the driveshaft, narrows to the tyres or a buckled rim.

cardLine: Whines on the overrun. Quiet under power.

resultCopy: Rock the propshaft by hand and there's play in a universal joint, fine rust-brown dust worked out round its cap. A worn prop UJ, not anything inside the diff.
The prop joints are tight, no rust dust at the caps, no play anywhere in the shaft. Not the propshaft, narrows to the diff's own bearings or its ring and pinion.
Metal glints on the swab off the diff's filler plug. A chewed ring and pinion, not tired bearings or a prop joint.
Off the filler plug the diff oil comes away clean, just thin with age. Not a chewed ring and pinion, narrows to worn diff bearings or a prop UJ.

cardLine: Sits lower than it should. Wallows over rough tarmac.

resultCopy: All four corners sit a little low and even, the coils sound and no corner out of line. Tired springs all round, not a break or a rotted mount.
One corner sits noticeably lower than the rest. Not even sag, narrows to a broken spring or a rotted strut top.
Up under the bonnet the strut top mount has rusted through, the turret flaking and the mount lifting under load. A rotted strut top, structural, not a spring fault.
The strut tops are solid, sound metal round every mount. Nothing structural, narrows to tired springs or a cracked coil.

cardLine: Faint filler line along a rear quarter panel.

resultCopy: A fresh weld bead runs along the rail and a jacking point sits not quite square. A structural rail repair, not just skin-deep panel work.
The rail underneath looks factory: no weld beads, no kinks, jacking points true. Whatever's been done is skin-deep, narrows to a respray or a filled rust patch.
Run a magnet along the quarter and it drops off over one patch where it holds firm everywhere else, filler packed too thick for steel to be behind it. A filled rust hole in the panel, not just paint.
The magnet holds firm the length of the quarter, sound steel under the paint the whole way. No rust hole in the skin, narrows to a plain respray or a repair down in the structure.

cardLine: Carpet's damp on the passenger side.

resultCopy: The header tank is low and the damp smells sweet, a greasy film misting the inside of the screen. The heater matrix weeping into the cabin, not rainwater getting in.
Coolant level holds and the damp is plain rainwater, no sweetness to it. Not the matrix, narrows to water finding a way in through the bulkhead.
A probe pushed at the bulkhead seam goes through flaking, laminated rust, the metal gone soft along the join. A rotten structural seam, not a tired grommet or a wet carpet from above.
The bulkhead and floor take a probe firm and solid, only light surface bloom where the damp sits. The structure's sound, narrows to the heater matrix or water past a perished grommet.

cardLine: Deep rasp under load, quiet at idle.

resultCopy: Traced down the pipe, the rasp fades near the manifold and picks up again at a joint further back. A blown flex joint, not the manifold itself.
Traced down the pipe, the rasp is loudest right at the manifold, a hard metallic edge to it. A cracked manifold, not a joint further back.

cardLine: Oil light flickers at hot idle. Goes out with revs.

resultCopy: A proper gauge on the sender port reads steady and correct at hot idle, climbing cleanly with revs. The sender is lying, not the oil: a tired sender, not a real pressure loss.
The gauge agrees with the light: pressure genuinely sags low at hot idle and barely lifts with revs. Real low pressure, not a lying sender, narrows to the pump or the bearings.
On the stethoscope the bottom end has a deep knock that loudens under load, and there's bearing swarf caught on the sump-plug magnet. Worn mains, not the oil pump or a lying sender.
The bottom end stays quiet and even under load, the sump plug clean of swarf. No bearing knock, narrows to a worn oil pump or a sender telling tales.

cardLine: Hesitates and flat-spots under hard acceleration. Smooth enough at a steady cruise.

resultCopy: A gauge tee'd into the fuel line sags well off spec the moment the throttle's blipped. Fuel starvation on the filter and pump side, not the cam timing.
The gauge tee'd into the fuel line holds steady through the blip, right on spec. Not fuel starvation, narrows to the cam timing, slack or skipped.
A cylinder reads flat, next to no compression, the chain jumped a tooth and the valves met the pistons. Bent valves, not a slack chain or a starved fuel line.
Compression reads even and healthy across every cylinder, the valves all sealing. No bent valves, narrows to a starved fuel line or a chain merely stretched.

cardLine: Steering feels vague, wants constant small corrections at speed.

resultCopy: Rocking the wheel at 3 and 9 with the front end up, the play's all out at the track-rod-end ball joints; the rack itself stays tight. Tired track-rod ends, not the rack.
Rocking the wheel at 3 and 9, the play sits right at the rack itself, not the outer joints. A worn rack, not just tired track-rod ends.
